prose.en:
SSH version 1 was found to have multiple vulnerabilities and was subsequently replaced by SSH version 2. As such, an organisation implementing SSH should disable the use of SSH version 1. In addition, several security risks exist when SSH is configured in an insecure manner. To mitigate these security risks, SSH should be configured as per the settings below.

The settings below are based on OpenSSH. An organisation using other implementations of SSH should adapt these settings to suit their SSH implementation.
